L1.area: Virtual machine runtime and bytecode interpreter infrastructure

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the JavaScript engine where, during construction of a runtime error message, a reference to a converted string buffer is invalidated by a subsequent string conversion that reuses the same temporary storage. An attacker can trigger this condition through crafted JavaScript that provokes a specific type error, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution. This is only exploitable if the application permits evaluation of untrusted JavaScript.

L3.area: Runtime error message formatting and string view UTF-16 conversion utilities in the VM interpreter layer
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the JavaScript engine where, during construction of a type error message for property assignment on a transient (non-extensible) object, a reference to a UTF-16 converted string buffer is invalidated by a subsequent string conversion that appends to the same temporary allocator. An attacker can trigger this condition through crafted JavaScript that provokes this specific error path, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution. This is only exploitable if the application permits evaluation of untrusted JavaScript.

ground_truth.reason: In the transient object property assignment error path, a single temporary allocator was shared between two UTF-16 string conversions, causing the first returned reference to be invalidated when the second conversion appended to the same allocator, resulting in use of freed memory.
